summary: Reviewed this tick's two new modules — notifications.js (TASK-305) and thumbnails.js (TASK-306), both VERIFIED. Both clean: notifications.js inserts every message (incl. file-supplied filenames) via textContent into createElement'd nodes, dismiss button is a labeled <button aria-label=Dismiss>, no innerHTML/eval; thumbnails.js sets labels via textContent, uses innerHTML only to clear (=''), and its one querySelector interpolates only the loop-integer pageNum (1..numPages) into the .pdf-page[data-page-number] selector — not user text, so no selector injection. The touched upload.js is unchanged in validation (extension+MIME+empty+50MB+%PDF magic bytes+filename sanitize); its new EventBus.emit(PDF_LOADING,{name}) is feedback-only, fires before validation, and the name only reaches a textContent toast — safe. event-bus.js just adds the PDF_LOADING name. No eval/Function/document.write/insertAdjacentHTML anywhere. Infra clean: nginx -t ok; full resource CSP + nosniff/X-Frame-Options/Referrer-Policy all live; sensitive paths (.md/.sh/.git/dotfiles) and a path-traversal probe (js/../CLAUDE.md) all 403; index 200 with .mjs text/javascript; 0 failed SSH auth in 24h; no secrets in git (only archived-log prose + a .gitignore *.secret pattern); HTTP-only :80 (TLS deferred to DNS cutover). One finding remains OPEN: SEC-002 (viewer.js renderAll has no page-count cap — PDF-bomb/memory DoS gap on the 1.6 GiB box), re-confirmed present this tick (viewer.js line 53 loops 1..numPages uncapped) and now COMPOUNDED — thumbnails.js (TASK-306) adds a second per-page canvas loop, doubling the memory footprint of an uncapped high-page-count PDF. Right fix is still a single cap at the load boundary in viewer.js. Flagged for developer per review-don't-rewrite; guide updated.

findings.title: No page-count cap at PDF load (PDF-bomb / memory-exhaustion gap) — now compounded by thumbnails.js
findings.reason: upload.js bounds file size (50 MB) and validates magic bytes, but nothing bounds page COUNT. viewer.js loadDocument()/renderAll() render every page into its own canvas (line ~53 loops 1..pdfDoc.numPages, no guard). As of 2026-06-08 thumbnails.js (TASK-306) ALSO loops 1..numPages rendering a second canvas per page, so a crafted high-page-count PDF under 50 MB now doubles the canvas/memory pressure on the 1.6 GiB box. Client-side only — affects only the uploader's own tab, no server impact. Violates the security-guide 'max 1000 pages' rule.
findings.next_action: Developer: in viewer.js loadDocument() after getDocument().promise resolves, reject/warn (and emit ERROR so the new notifications toast surfaces it) when doc.numPages > 1000, or render lazily/windowed. A single cap at the load boundary protects every downstream per-page consumer (viewer + thumbnails + future features) at once — do not add per-feature caps. Documented in docs/security-guide.md (KNOWN GAP block). Not implemented by security per review-don't-rewrite.

code_review.notifications_xss: all toast text (incl. file-supplied filenames) via textContent on createElement'd nodes; dismiss is <button aria-label=Dismiss>; no innerHTML with content — OK
code_review.thumbnails_xss_and_selector: labels via textContent; innerHTML only used to clear (''); goToPage querySelector interpolates only loop-integer pageNum (1..numPages), not user text — no selector injection — OK
code_review.upload_validation: magic-bytes + MIME + .pdf extension + empty-check + 50MB limit + filename sanitize — OK (unchanged); new PDF_LOADING emit is feedback-only, pre-validation, name reaches textContent only
code_review.event_bus_change: added PDF_LOADING canonical event name only — OK
code_review.xss_sinks: all innerHTML occurrences (toc.js/metadata.js/viewer.js/thumbnails.js) are = '' clears; no eval/Function/document.write/insertAdjacentHTML — OK
code_review.page_count_cap: MISSING — see SEC-002 (now compounded by thumbnails.js)

headers_live: Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'wasm-unsafe-eval'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' blob: data:; worker-src 'self' blob:; font-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' blob:; frame-ancestors 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'
tls: none (HTTP-only on :80; certbot deferred until cronloop.techtools.cz DNS cutover)
